params.name: Babylonian Clay Tablet
params.description:
On Jul 6, 1990 AD, archaeologists from Yale University discovered a clay tablet at the Ruins of Babylon.
This clay tablet is the world's earliest known cookbook, recording several ancient Babylonian cuisines, including a salad, a thick soup, and a lamb broth. The tablet details the ingredients used in cooking at the time, including onions, carrots, and oats. Some chefs have recreated these dishes according to the instructions on the tablet, and one of them has even become a signature dish of …
